other_observers: Bob Zilly was with me.; No, I am not. Linda Andes-Georges found the bird on 5 June 2009, and provisionally ID'd the bird as a Kentucky. Soon thereafter, the bird was re-found (by Nathan Pieplow?), and its ID as a Kentucky Warbler was confirmed.
duration: heard for 30+ seconds, seen for ~3 seconds
description: I saw the bird very briefly. This bird was a "stakeout," seen previously, and copiously photographed by other birders. For this submission, I am submitting only documentation pertaining to the bird's vocalizations.Behaviors: The bird called frequently while it was skulking in dense understory vegetation. It responded to pishing, came out for a brief view, then disappeared.Call: Called frequently. I am uploading two recordings of the bird calling. The call was a loud, hollow, rather "wooden" clucking sound.Plumage: alternate
photographer: Photographer: Bob Zilly, Ted Floyd, BOB ZILLY
